seo_h1: The Trojan War in the Fragments Attributed to Dictys Cretensis

seo.browser_title: Dictys Cretensis on Eulogikon Greek Archive and Library
seo.meta_title: Classical Dictys Cretensis Ancient Greek Historical Works
seo.meta_description: Dictys Cretensis, a Roman era historian writing in Koine Greek. Two surviving historical works. Free to read and download from the Greek library archive.

biography_html:
Dictys Cretensis is the pseudonymous author of the Ephemeris belli Troiani, or "Journal of the Trojan War." No reliable biographical details exist. According to the work's fictional preface, he was a Cretan companion of Idomeneus at Troy who recorded an eyewitness account, a claim which is a literary fabrication. The actual Latin prose work was likely composed in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

His sole known work is the Ephemeris belli Troiani, a prose narrative of the Trojan War. It was originally composed in Greek, but survives primarily in a 4th-century CE Latin translation, with only a fragment of the Greek original extant.

The Ephemeris is a key text in the ancient alternative tradition of the Trojan War, offering a pro-Greek, ostensibly historical account distinct from Homeric epic. Though a pseudepigraphon, it was widely treated as genuine history in late antiquity and the Middle Ages. Its Latin version was profoundly influential on medieval European literature, shaping the Trojan narratives of authors such as Benoît de Sainte-Maure, Guido delle Colonne, and Chaucer.
